content: Just as the linguistic sense of reflexion taught in high school English curricula frames it as both a reflection of internal thought and a mirrored reflection of external input, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to explicitly build this double-layered reflexion: the LLM not only reflects on its own problem-solving output, but also reflects on how its output maps to the implicit intent behind the user's input, similar to how language learners practice reflexion to connect their own writing to the context of a prompt. This double-layered structure creates more aligned and self-correcting outputs than current single-perspective LLM reflection frameworks.
